title: MCP profile stack stabilization audit report
description: The single JSON document produced by `python scripts/run-mcp-profile-stack-stabilization-audit.py --json` (E22): a read-only consistency map of the MCP profile stack across six dimensions (refusal-code registry, CLI taxonomy, schema inventory, docs map, audit field names, security boundaries). Findings carry severities info/warning/problem; exit code 0 means no problems (warnings allowed). Subjects and messages are repo-relative names, command paths, field names, or code numbers only -- never absolute local paths, key material, or hashes.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp of report generation -- the only wall-clock field; everything else is deterministic for the same repository state.

mode.description: `fixture` when --fixture-mode was given (determinism pinned for CI); the audit is read-only over the repository either way.

dimensions.description: One entry per audited dimension, in the fixed audit order.

ok.description: True exactly when no problem-severity finding exists (warnings allowed).

checks.description: How many individual assertions this dimension ran (a finding-free check is silent).

findings.description: Sorted: problems first, then warnings, then info; stable within a severity.

check.description: Machine name of the assertion that produced this finding.

subject.description: What the finding is about: a repo-relative path (forward slashes), an `mcp ...` command path, a flag, a field name, or a refusal code. Never an absolute local path.
